subject: Geography
title: Kinshasa and Brazzaville: Twin Cities
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Kinshasa and Brazzaville: Twin Cities.

hook.text: Kinshasa and Brazzaville face each other across the Congo River—the closest two capitals on Earth.

buildup.text: Kinshasa has exploded to 17 million people, making it Africa's third-largest city.

discovery.text: The two cities were named after rival European explorers—Stanley and de Brazza.

twist.text: Despite being just 1.6 km apart, crossing between them requires visas and a ferry.

climax.text: A bridge has been planned for decades but politics on both sides keep delaying it.

punchline.text: Two capitals stare at each other but can barely shake hands.
